title: Hue rotation sweep on solid source

metricComparisons.description: rotating hue by ~1/3 of a turn should rotate the red solid out of frame and drop meanR
rotating hue by ~1/3 of a turn should rotate green into the rendered frame and lift meanG

expectedVideo: amount=0.33 should rotate the red solid to a green-leaning hue (meanR↓, meanG↑)
amount=1.0 should return close to the original red hue (full rotation)
expectedAudio: amount=0.333 should raise the rendered pitch by ~+400 cents (audible on the solid triad)
amount=1.0 sits at the pitch-shifter worklet edge (ratio=2); the +1-octave checkpoint stays manual-listen because the worklet degrades at the limit
amount=0 should be inaudibly identical to bypass

Audio side is MANUAL REVIEW ONLY (no hard gate): the solid source generates audio at the noise floor (~-173 dB on the exported WAV), so exported-audio metrics like zeroCrossingRate are dominated by quantisation noise rather than pitch content — observed ZCR delta sign-flipped between runs (+0.00126 then -0.00129) at the same operator settings, confirming it. Same precedent as audit-colorama-solid-sweep / audit-modulate-osc-sweep / audit-scrollX-osc-sweep. The .webm captures the full sweep for listening.
manualChecks: Does the hue ring traverse smoothly without colour-banding artefacts at small amount?
Is the pitch shift musically intelligible across the ±1 octave range, or does the worklet introduce zipper/aliasing noise?
Do the visual hue motion and the audio pitch motion feel locked rather than co-incidental?
